text.title: X / Twitter Ads Strategy Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ads-twitter-ads methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: X paid campaigns MUST target B2B / tech / crypto / media-adjacent ICPs. Mass-consumer verticals are forbidden — channel does not pay back.
rule.rationale: Post-2023 X user base concentrates in these verticals; broader verticals see 3-5x CPA vs Meta.

rule.statement: Every campaign MUST attach a content-category exclusion list (politics, hate, harassment, sensitive news).
rule.rationale: X relaxed moderation post-2023; brand-adjacency risk is high without explicit excludes.

rule.statement: Promoted-tweet copy MUST contain ≤2 emojis. >2 lowers CTR and triggers spam classifier.
rule.rationale: Internal CTR audits + X engagement studies.

rule.statement: Targeting MUST be Follower-Lookalike (≥1000 source) OR keyword/conversation. Broad interest-only targeting is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Broad interest CPM is 2-3x; LAL + keyword are the only audiences that price competitively.

rule.statement: Reports MUST include viewability rate; campaigns with viewability <50% are paused for placement audit.
rule.rationale: X has higher placement waste than Meta; viewability is the only honest delivery gate.
